item.title: Utility companies to spend $1.4 trillion over the next 5 years amid data center boom - CBS News
item.description: U.S. investor-owned utilities plan to invest $1.4 trillion over the next five years, a 20% increase from 2025 projections, primarily driven by data center electricity demand. This capital expenditure surge risks passing significant costs to residential ratepayers, with oversight needed to manage the financial impact.

item.title: Data centers undertake great Midwest migration in search of power - Fierce Network
item.description: Data center developers are shifting major projects to the Midwest, particularly Illinois, due to severe power constraints in traditional hubs. This migration is creating new grid demands and opportunities for large-scale power infrastructure, including generation and storage.

item.title: Utility investment plans jump 21%, further threatening affordability: PowerLines
item.description: On 2026-04-15, Utility Dive reported on rising utility investment plans and rate increase requests, driven by load growth from data centers and AI, which threaten electricity affordability. Utility capital expenditure plans jumped 21%, with $31 billion in rate increases requested in the latest year, and summer peak demand is projected to grow by 224 GW (24%) over the next decade.

item.title: Apollo's Jim Zelter on AI Disruption and the Future of Private Credit
item.description: On 2026-04-16, Apollo Global Management President Jim Zelter discussed investment trends in a Goldman Sachs interview recorded on March 11, 2026. No specific numbers (MW/MWh/$/rate/deadline) were provided in the available content.

item.title: Texas power demand to more than quadruple by 2032 as data center boom rolls on, ERCOT says - San Antonio Express-News
item.description: ERCOT forecasts Texas peak electricity demand will more than quadruple to 376,790 MW by 2032, driven primarily by data centers. This unprecedented load growth presents a massive grid capacity and reliability challenge for the state.

item.title: Data Centers, Power Markets, and the Economy: How the AI Buildout is Reshaping Energy Costs and the Grid - JD Supra
item.description: Data center and AI-driven electricity demand is materially impacting wholesale power prices, retail rates, and grid interconnection queues, with PJM's 2026 capacity auction already causing double-digit retail price increases in states like New Jersey. This has triggered federal and state policy responses, including the White House's March 2026 'Ratepayer Protection Pledge' and new state-level large-load tariffs.

item.title: Energy demand in ComEd territory could double by 2040, in part driven by data centers - Capitol News Illinois
item.description: Electricity demand in ComEd's service territory could more than double by 2040, primarily driven by data center projects in the interconnection queue. This surge creates an urgent need for new generation and demand management solutions to prevent supply shortfalls.

item.title: Energy demand in ComEd territory could double by 2040, in part driven by data centers - Daily Herald
item.description: Electricity demand in ComEd's service territory is projected to double by 2040, with data center growth cited as a key driver. This scale of demand growth will require significant new grid infrastructure and generation capacity.
